The letter was issued in response to the Trump administration’s pressure campaign against a proposed Utah bill that would require AI companies to be more transparent about how they strive to protect children using their systems and how they plan to limit catastrophic risks from their models, such as assisting terrorists in the creation of bioweapons or crippling cyberattacks.
Friday’s proposed framework says that states should retain the power to prosecute issues that would normally fall under state jurisdiction, such as preventing fraud and protecting consumers.

The policy document also prioritizes protections against AI-related censorship, advocating that Congress should prevent the federal government "from coercing technology providers, including AI providers, to ban, compel, or alter content based on partisan or ideological agendas."

This anti-censorship messaging comes shortly after Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth cut off Anthropic, one of America's leading AI companies, from government business for being "woke" and misaligned with government priorities.
Anthropic is now suing the federal government, claiming that the abrupt cancellation of its work with the government infringed on its First Amendment rights.

title: 3 injured as turbulence hits Delta flight landing in Sydney
summary: Three people were taken to hospital after a Delta Air Lines flight encountered turbulence shortly before landing in Sydney, health officials in Australia said.
story: Three people were taken to a hospital after a Delta Air Lines flight  from Los Angeles encountered turbulence shortly before landing in Sydney on Friday, health officials in Australia said.
Paramedics assessed five people and transported three to the city's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital for further treatment, a spokesperson for the New South Wales Ambulance Service confirmed to NBC News.
They suffered “minor injuries” and remained in stable condition, the spokesperson added.
“The patients have asked that no details be provided to the media,” a spokesperson for the Sydney Local Health District said in a separate email.
“The most common cause for turbulence on flights around Sydney is convective storms,” according to Michael Heisel, a lecturer at The University of Sydney's School of Civil Engineering.
“These weather events are becoming more common in Australia with the warming climate,” he said in an email Friday.
Flight-tracking data published by Flightradar24 showed the plane from Los Angeles landed just after 6.40 a.m. local time (3:40 p.m. Thursday ET).

The flight “encountered brief turbulence upon descent into Sydney,” a Delta spokesperson said in an email. “Four flight attendants reported injuries, three of whom sought further evaluation, and all have since been released.”
There were 245 passengers and 15 crew members aboard the flight, the Delta spokesperson said. None of the passengers reported injuries, they added.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
Turbulence is common on many flights and over the past year several people have been injured as planes from multiple airlines have encountered it.

In July, 25 people were taken to hospital after a Delta flight heading from Utah to Amsterdam experienced “significant turbulence” and had to land in Minneapolis. And in September, three flight attendants also suffered minor injuries during a turbulent Delta flight from Ecuador to Atlanta.

title: Taylor Frankie Paul's 'Bachelorette' Contestants React to News of the Show's Cancellation
summary: The men who were on Taylor Frankie Paul's season of The Bachelorette are reacting to the show's cancellation. Here's exactly what they said.
story: Some of the men featured on Taylor Frankie Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” are responding to the news that ABC has canceled the season just days before its March 22 premiere.

The cancellation came following the resurfacing of a video depicting Paul assaulting her ex-boyfriend, Dakota Mortensen on March 19. The same day, the network announced that Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” would not be airing.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson said in a statement given to TODAY.com.

The video, published by TMZ, depicts Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter. The video is reportedly from 2023. Mortensen appears to be the person filming the altercation. NBC News has not independently verified the video or viewed the original video.

Paul was arrested and charged with three misdemeanors in 2023: assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child, according to Salt Lake County Sheriff’s Office records obtained by NBC News.
She pleaded guilty to abeyance to aggravated assault in August 2023, per court records. The other charges against her were dropped.

Following the release of the video, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul on March 19, according to documents obtained by NBC News. The filing was then sent back for a correction and an amended petition.
Paul’s spokesperson issued a statement March 19 given to TODAY.com.

“Taylor is very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security. After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm.”

The statement continued, “There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives. Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”
NBC News has reached out to Mortensen for comment.
Now, the cast is reacting to learning their season won’t air.
How the Cast of 'The Bachelorette' Season 22 Has Responded

Matt Carroll, a 43-year-old real estate broker, took to his Instagram story to share a video from The Indianapolis Star, a news outlet in Indiana. In the video, a reporter broke down the news of the show's cancellation.
Below the video, Carroll wrote, "On pause for now."
"Thank you for everyone's support and thoughtful messages," he wrote, adding heart and rose emojis.
Malik Evans, a 30-year-old tech executive from New York, posted the following update to his Instagram story on March 19.
"I'm doing okay. Hope to share more soon. Thank you to everyone who has reached out," he wrote, also adding heart and rose emojis.
Mike Turitto, a 36-year-old brand protection manager from New Jersey, took a subtle approach.
On the morning of March 20, the Season 22 contestant posted a view from his bedroom window and captured a beautiful sunrise. He captioned the Instagram story, "It's a new day."

Conrad Ukropina, a 32-year-old startup founder from California, posted a photo of himself with Paul during filming of Season 22 in his Instagram story. He set the photo to the Miley Cyrus song, "Slide Away."
In the clip that he shared, Cyrus sings the following lyrics:
Once upon a time it was paradise
Once upon a time I was paralyzed
Think I’m gonna miss these harbour lights
But it’s time to let it go
Once upon a time it was made for us
Woke up one day, it had turned to dust
Baby, we were found, but now we’re lost
So it’s time to let it go
Clayton Johnson, a 36-year-old singer/songwriter from Tennessee,  spoke about the bonds formed in the Bachelor Mansion.

"I can honestly say this was one of the best groups of men I've ever been around. So much integrity, respect, and heart across the board. Even the tough moments never took away from that," he wrote. "Any woman would be lucky to be with any of these guys. Real integrity. Real respect. Real men."
Johnson ended his post by writing, "Love you boys," and shared a wilting rose emoji.
Doug Mason, a 28-year-old ocean lifeguard from California, shared a video message on the morning of March 20 on his Instagram story.

"Good morning everybody. In light of everything that's happened, I'm just sending out prayers to Taylor because that was her moment, and her moment was blocked. So all we can do right now is just be hopeful," he said.
Mason ended his message on a positive light, saying, "Let's stay positive and show nothing but support for people in need."
Johnnie LaRossa
Johnnie LaRossa, a baseball player from Long Island, responded to the news with an ode to the men on the show.

"Appreciate everyone reaching out. Grateful for the experience. And I've gotta say the group of guys were truly unbelievable. Nothing but respect for them. Love you boys and any woman would be lucky to have each and every one of them."

title: Chuck Norris, Martial Artist and Action Film Icon, Dies At 86
summary: "Walker, Texas Ranger" star Chuck Norris has died, his family announced March 20, less than two weeks after he turned 86.
story: Action star Chuck Norris has died, his family announced March 20. He was 86.

“It is with heavy hearts that our family shares the sudden passing of our beloved Chuck Norris yesterday morning,” his family wrote in an Instagram post accompanied by a black and white picture of the actor.

“While we would like to keep the circumstances private, please know that he was surrounded by his family and was at peace. To the world, he was a martial artist, actor, and a symbol of strength. To us, he was a devoted husband, a loving father and grandfather, an incredible brother, and the heart of our family.”
The post praised the love he tried to spread throughout his life.

“He lived his life with faith, purpose, and an unwavering commitment to the people he loved. Through his work, discipline, and kindness, he inspired millions around the world and left a lasting impact on so many lives,” the family wrote.

“While our hearts are broken, we are deeply grateful for the life he lived and for the unforgettable moments we were blessed to share with him. The love and support he received from fans around the world meant so much to him, and our family is truly thankful for it. To him, you were not just fans, you were his friends.”

The "Walker, Texas Ranger" star was recently hospitalized in Hawaii, Variety reported. In the statement, the family referenced his “recent hospitalization" without providing further details and said they “are truly grateful for the prayers and support you sent his way.”
“Thank you for loving him with us. With love, The Norris Family,” the post ended.
Norris turned 86 on March 10. He posted a video on Instagram of him boxing to commemorate the birthday.
“I don’t age. I level up,” he said in the clip and wrote in the caption. “I’m 86 today!” he continued his caption.

“Nothing like some playful action on a sunny day to make you feel young. I’m grateful for another year, good health and the chance to keep doing what I love. Thank you all for being the best fans in the world. Your support through the years has meant more to me than you’ll ever know.”

An Air Force veteran, Norris was a martial arts icon who founded the Chuck Norris System, "a Korean-based American hard style of karate,” that would also be known as Chun Kuk Do, according to the United Fighting Arts Federation.

Norris would eventually make the transition to Hollywood, with his film debut in 1968’s “The Wrecking Crew.” He would go on to star alongside Bruce Lee in the 1972 movie “The Way of the Dragon” before landing a string of action films, including “Breaker! Breaker!,” “The Delta Force,” the “Missing in Action” franchise, “Good Guys Wear Black,” “The Octagon,” “Code of Silence,” “Lone Wolf McQuade,” “Invasion U.S.A.,” “Firewalker” and “Sidekicks.”

Norris would transition to TV, playing former Marine Cordell Walker in CBS’ action series “Walker, Texas Ranger.” The show was a huge hit, running for nine seasons from 1993 until 2001. He would also return to the character in a pair of TV movies, with the last one, “Walker, Texas Ranger: Trial by Fire,” airing in 2005.
Norris did not appear on screen often in his final years, although he did appear in 2012’s “The Expendables 2.” His last credit was 2024’s “Agent Recon,” according to IMDB.
Norris’ image also became fodder for memes in the Internet age, with his face often appearing over fake facts to prove how tough he is.

“Chuck Norris kills 100% of germs,” “In an emergency, 911 calls him,” “When the Hulk gets really angry, he turns into Chuck Norris,” were among the phrases that have made the rounds on the internet over the years.

title: Nicole Scherzinger and Pussycat Dolls Respond to Comments From Former Members Left Out of Reunion Tour
summary: Nicole Scherzinger and Pussycat Dolls respond to criticism from former members Jessica Sutta and Carmit Bachar who are not included in the reunion tour.
story: Members of the Pussycat Dolls are speaking out about their decision to return to touring as a trio.
During a live interview on the 3rd Hour of TODAY on March 20, Craig Melvin asked Nicole Scherzinger, Kimberly Wyatt and Ashley Roberts about the decision to tour as a group of three, not six.
Original members Carmit Bachar, Melody Thornton and Jessica Sutta are not part of the tour, which starts in June.

"As you probably know, some former members of the Pussycat Dolls, they’ve made some headlines recently after they found out that you guys were coming back as a trio, not as the original group. How did you arrive at that decision?" TODAY's Craig Melvin asked.
Scherzinger began to respond, saying, "Well, I mean, listen, we are just, we are like, we are so. As women today...."
Wyatt interjected and shared her perspective.

“I mean, it has been an ever-changing lineup and you know, this is what it looks like now in 2026, and you never know what comes next. I think ultimately we’ve got to protect our peace and when something like the Pussycat Dolls has so much history, we have ruptured in the past, and right now we are repairing, and we’re sort of on the same page with that," she said.

Following up on what her bandmate said, Scherzinger spoke again, saying, "But what I wanted to say was that we have so much love and respect for all of the women that have been part of the PCD legacy. And once a doll, always a doll."
Former Pussycat Dolls member Carmit Bachar, who is not part of the reunion tour, shared her reaction to the tour in an Instagram post. She said she was not aware of the band's plans ahead of time.

"In light of recent developments, I feel it is important to speak honestly and respectfully. I was not contacted regarding the group’s decision to move forward, and I learned of these plans at the same time as the public. Given my history with the brand, having been part of its foundation long before its commercial debut and instrumental in the connections that led to the record deal…I would have appreciated direct communication," she wrote.
Bachar called the experience "disappointing on a personal level" but said she remains "proud" of the role she played in "helping shape what The Pussycat Dolls became."

"My intention in sharing this is not to create division, but to honor the truth and the fans who have supported us throughout the years. Transparency and respect are values I hold deeply. At this time, I am choosing to focus on my well-being and to move forward with positivity and purpose. I remain open-hearted about the future and grateful for the continued love and encouragement I receive," she wrote.

Jessica Sutta also responded on Instagram with a lengthy statement. Sutta said she learned of the reunion "the night before the announcement" and didn't feel like she had "much time to prepare for the reaction that followed online."
"I simply would have appreciated a heads-up earlier so I could process everything privately and handle the situation with a bit more grace and dignity," she said.
Sutta said she wished the touring members "a successful tour and nothing but the best moving forward."

In the caption, she wrote, “I’ve moved on with my life from Hollyweird and I know what’s most important. A loving family, good health, beautiful friendships, speaking your truth, lots of laughter & internal peace. And if we are really being honest, you can’t trip on what’s behind you. Good luck, ladies. All love.”

title: Taylor Frankie Paul Speaks Out After ‘Bachelorette’ Season Pulled: ‘Preparing to Own and Share Her Story’
summary: Taylor Frankie Paul is speaking out following news that her season of “The Bachelorette” has been pulled from the air.
story: Taylor Frankie Paul is speaking out following news that her season of “The Bachelorette” has been canceled.

A statement from Paul’s spokesperson obtained by TODAY.com on March 19 reads, “Taylor is very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security. After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm.”

The statement continued, “There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives. Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”

Her response comes hours after Disney announced it has pulled Season 22 of “The Bachelorette,” which was set to premiere on ABC March 22 with Paul as the lead, due to an investigation involving “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star and her ex, Dakota Mortensen, and a resurfaced video released by TMZ hours before.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson said in a statement sent to TODAY.com.

Disney’s statement is referring to a 2023 video TMZ shared March 19 that shows Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter. Mortensen appears to be the one filming the altercation.

“It’s sad to see the latest installment of his never-ending, desperate, attention-seeking, destructive campaign to harm Taylor without any regard for the consequences for their child,” Paul’s representative said in a statement to NBC News regarding the video. “Releasing an old video, which conveniently omits context, on their son’s birthday is a reprehensible attempt to distract from his own behavior.”
Mortensen responded in a statement to People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” he said. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”
Additionally, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul on March 19 in Salt Lake County, documents obtained by NBC News confirm. A court date has been scheduled for April 7.

Before her “Bachelorette” season was canceled, it was reported earlier this week that filming for “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” had been paused amid an investigation into Paul and Mortensen, who share a 2-year-old son.

Three sources familiar with the situation confirmed to NBC News March 17 that Paul and Mortensen were part of an “ongoing investigation.” The sources commented on the condition of anonymity and did not provide more details.

People reported March 16 that the Draper City Police Department told the outlet Paul and Mortensen were connected to a “domestic assault investigation.” The spokesperson added that “allegations have been made in both directions” and “contact was made with involved parties” on Feb. 24 and 25.
However, the department declined to confirm the report to NBC News. “It is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations,” the department said.

title: ABC Pulls ‘Bachelorette’ Season Amid Investigation Surrounding Taylor Frankie Paul
summary: Season 22 of “The Bachelorette” has been canceled amid a police investigation involving its lead, reality TV star Taylor Frankie Paul
story: Season 22 of “The Bachelorette” has been canceled amid a police investigation involving its lead, reality TV star Taylor Frankie Paul.

On March 19, just three days before the reality dating show was set to premiere on ABC, the network’s parent company, Disney, announced the show’s fate following headlines involving Paul and her ex, Dakota Mortensen.
Disney addressed its decision in a statement to TODAY.com.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson said.
Following the cancellation, a spokesperson for Paul told NBC News that the reality star is “very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security.”

“After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm,” the statement reads. “There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives.

“Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story,” the statements concludes.

Hours before Disney's announcement, TMZ published a video reportedly from 2023 that shows Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter. Mortensen appears to be the one filming the altercation. NBC News has not independently verified the video or viewed the original video.
Paul’s representative spoke to the video’s release in a statement to NBC News.

“It’s sad to see the latest installment of his never-ending, desperate, attention-seeking, destructive campaign to harm Taylor without any regard for the consequences for their child,” the statement said, speaking of Mortensen.
Mortensen responded in a statement to People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” he said. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”

On March 19, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul in Salt Lake County, documents obtained by NBC News confirm. The filing was sent back for a correction and an amended petition.

Paul is known as one of the creators of #MomTok, a group of Utah moms who create viral dance-based TikTok videos. She is among a group of women who star on “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” which just dropped for a fourth season on Hulu.

Disney decision to scrap “The Bachelorette” came after reports this week that “Mormon Wives” halted filming Season 5 amid an investigation into Paul and Mortensen, and hours after TMZ resurfaced what it said is a two-year-old video of Paul attacking Mortensen.

On March 17, three sources familiar with the matter confirmed to NBC News that Paul and Mortensen are part of an “ongoing investigation” and that “Mormon Wives” had stopped filming during the probe. The sources, who commented on the condition of anonymity, declined to provide further information.

On March 16, the Draper City Police Department told People that Paul and Mortensen, who share a 2-year-old child, are connected to an open “domestic assault investigation.” The spokesperson said “allegations have been made in both directions” and “contact was made with involved parties” on Feb. 24 and 25.
The department declined to confirm the report to NBC News, stating, “it is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations.”
While attending a press preview for her season of “The Bachelorette” on March 17, the reality star spoke out about the investigation for the first time.

“Honestly, just like, my heart hurts to see it, to go through it, especially at this time,” she said. “Just the timing is hard, and it’s a big deal. I feel like every premiere that I’ve experienced, I’ve never enjoyed fully, so this is another one... it’s extremely hard, and it took everything to get me here today.”
Mortensen’s representative said in statement that “his number one priority here is protecting (their 2-year-old son), Ever.”
Paul, a mother of three, also shares two children, an 8-year-old daughter and a 5-year-old son, with her ex-husband, Tate Paul.

Taylor Frankie Paul was arrested in 2023 after a fight with Mortensen, which was documented in the first episode of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives.” She was charged with three misdemeanors: assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child. She later reached a plea deal in her assault case, pleading guilty in abeyance to aggravated assault in August 2023, court documents show. The other charges against her were dropped.

title: What’s Happening with Taylor Frankie Paul? ABC Pulls Her ‘Bachelorette’ Season Amid Police Investigation
summary: Taylor Frankie Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” will not air amid an “ongoing investigation” involving the “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star and her ex, Dakota Mortensen
story: Taylor Frankie Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” will not air amid an “ongoing investigation” involving the “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star and her ex, Dakota Mortensen.
After TMZ published a video reportedly from an altercation between Paul and Mortensen in 2023 that led to her arrest, Disney announced the cancellation on March 19.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney Entertainment Television spokesperson said in a statement.
Paul said in a statement via her spokesperson that she's "very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security."

Paul and Mortensen are currently part of an “ongoing investigation,” three sources close to the matter told NBC News March 17. The sources, who commented on the condition of anonymity, declined to provide further information about the investigation.

Draper City Police Department told People on March 16 that Paul and Mortensen, who share a 2-year-old son, are connected to an open “domestic assault investigation.” (The department declined to confirm the report to NBC News, stating, “it is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations.”)

Paul, one of the stars of the Hulu reality series “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” is known as one of the creators of #MomTok, group of Utah moms who have amassed a massive following for their dance-based TikTok videos.

In 2024, a wider audience was introduced to MomTok when Hulu premiered a reality series that gave an inside look at the lives of the group’s members. Their spotlight only grew when cast members Jen Affleck and Whitney Leavitt competed in Season 34 of “Dancing with the Stars,” while Paul was announced as the Season 22 lead of “The Bachelorette,” which was supposed to premiere March 22, just 10 days after Season 4 of “Mormon Wives” dropped on Hulu.
Filming for Season 5 of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” has been halted amid the investigation, the three sources close to the matter told NBC News.
Here’s what we know about the investigation involving the mother of three and its impact on “The Bachelorette.”
'The Bachelorette' Cancellation Comes After the Release of a 2023 Video

In 2023, Paul was arrested and charged with three misdemeanors: assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child, according to Salt Lake County Sheriff’s Office records obtained by NBC News.

Paul was arrested after a fight with Mortensen, which was documented in the first episode of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives.” She later reached a plea deal in her assault case, pleading guilty in abeyance to aggravated assault in August 2023, court records show. The other charges against her were dropped.

On March 19, TMZ published a video reportedly connected to the 2023 assault case. In the video, Paul is shown attacking Mortensen with a chair in front of her daughter. NBC News has not independently verified the circumstances and authenticity of the video.
Paul’s rep responded to its release in a statement to NBC News.

“It’s sad to see the latest installment of his never-ending, desperate, attention-seeking, destructive campaign to harm Taylor without any regard for the consequences for their child,” the statement said, speaking of Mortensen. “Releasing an old video, which conveniently omits context, on their son’s birthday is a reprehensible attempt to distract from his own behavior. Thankfully, the public has seen this act before and knows who he is and sadly, many will recognize this pattern of manipulation, both in his actions on the show, and from their own experiences.”
“I will have my truth,” Paul said in a phone interview with NBC News after the video was released.
Mortensen responded in a statement to People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” he said. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”

On March 19, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul in Salt Lake County, documents obtained by NBC News confirm. The filing was sent back for a correction and an amended petition.
What Has Taylor Frankie Paul Said About the Cancellation

After Disney announced it was pulling her season of “The Bachelorette,” her representative said in a statement that Paul is “currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”

“After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm,” the statement read.

“There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives. Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming,” she added.
NBC News has reached out to Mortensen for comment.
Why Is Taylor Frankie Paul Part of an Investigation?

NBC News spoke with three sources, who commented on the condition of anonymity, and confirmed that Paul is involved in an “ongoing investigation.” The sources declined to provide further details about the investigation.

Paul attended a press preview for her season of “The Bachelorette” on March 17 and called the situation a “heavy time” to People. Mortensen’s representative released a statement to Entertainment Weekly, saying, “His number one priority here is protecting (their son), Ever.”

On March 16, a spokesperson for the Draper City Police Department in Utah told People there was an open "domestic assault investigation" related to Paul and Mortensen. The spokesperson said "allegations have been made in both directions" and "contact was made with involved parties" on Feb. 24 and 25.
Asked to confirm the report, the Draper Police Department told NBC News it would not release details related to active investigations.

The West Jordan Police Department in Utah told NBC News March 18 that it received a domestic violence allegation involving Paul. The department did not know the date or time of the reported incident, and it declined to give further details.
Disney, the parent company of Hulu and ABC — which air “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” and “The Bachelorette,” respectively — has not responded to NBC News’ request for comment on the investigation.
It cited the release of the video reportedly from a 2023 incident as the reason for pulling Season 22 of “The Bachelorette.”
What Has Taylor Frankie Paul Said Amid the Investigation?
On March 17, Paul spoke with People about the ongoing investigation during a press preview for “The Bachelorette.”

“Honestly, just like, my heart hurts to see it, to go through it, especially at this time,” she said. “Just the timing is hard, and it’s a big deal. I feel like every premiere that I’ve experienced, I’ve never enjoyed fully, so this is another one... it’s extremely hard, and it took everything to get me here today.”
In a separate interview with Entertainment Weekly, Paul said she has had “better days.” She also discussed the status of her co-parenting relationship with Mortensen.
“I actually took action about a few weeks ago,” she said. “I’m just doing third-party everything — pick-up, drop-offs, communication — so no contact right now for me.”
“I think that’s the best case, honestly, because clearly it’s just not to a point of us getting along, so I wanted to take the best action I could,” she added.
While speaking about the situation, Paul addressed the public nature of her life.
“I didn’t want to bring anything to the public eye, making it a bigger deal for the sake of my son, and just everything about to come out. But that wasn’t the plan for him,” she said.
Paul has three children. She shares an 8-year-old daughter and 5-year-old son with her ex-husband, Tate Paul. Her son with Mortensen turned 2 on March 19.
What Has Dakota Mortensen Said Amid the Investigation?
On March 18, a representative for Mortensen addressed the investigation in a statement shared with Entertainment Weekly.
“His number one priority here is protecting (their son), Ever,” the statement reads.
Mortensen’s representative also denied that he is responsible for the investigation becoming public.

“He knew there was a possibility it could come out, but he was not going to be the one to proactively do that because he has always wanted a decent relationship with Taylor. It’s been really hard to achieve that, but he wants to co-parent well,” the statement continues.
Per the reality star’s representative, Mortensen felt compelled to comment publicly given the gravity of the situation.

“He was just hoping that if he says nothing, as he usually does, it would go away. He’s never done any kind of sit-down interview about his side,” the representative said. “He kind of just lets it all happen to him, and I think he realizes with the severity of everything now that he just can’t do that.”
What To Know About Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen’s Relationship
Viewers of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” have followed Paul and Mortensen’s on-again-off-again relationship.
Over the last few years, Paul and Mortensen have broken up and gotten back together on several occasions.
In Season 4 of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” Paul prepared for her “Bachelorette” journey and spent time apart from Mortensen. She even explored a romance with a new man.

During one episode, however, the exes slept together while the cast rented a house together in Los Angeles to support their co-stars on “Dancing With the Stars.” They had another tryst when Paul dropped their son off at Mortensen’s the night before she left to film “The Bachelorette.”
In an interview with Us Weekly, Paul said she and Mortensen had been stuck in a “cycle.”
“It’s been really hard to get out … It’s been so hard to remove myself from that,” she said.
When asked how she reassured the “Bachelorette” contestants that she was ready to move on from her ex, Paul said it was a “choice.”
“Ready, at the end of the day, is a decision. So I made the decision to leave for two months and try. I reassured them that was why I was here,” she said.
What’s Happening with ‘The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’?
In the wake of the investigation involving Paul and Mortensen, filming for Season 5 has been paused, three sources close to the matter told NBC News.
Paul appeared on “Good Morning America” March 18 and was asked to confirm the pause in production.

“I don’t call the shots with production,” she said. “So as of now, I have gotten word that it also has been paused, but I do not call those shots. So as far as I know, it was, but I don’t know for how long and why.”
Mikayla Matthews, who has starred on “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” since Season 1, responded to questions from fans on Instagram March 18, including one that read, “Is filming actually on pause??”
“It was a decision that all us girls came up with and agreed on,” Matthews wrote. “We didn’t feel comfortable filming with everything that was happening.”

title: How Did JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette React to Princess Diana's Death?
summary: Did JFK Jr call Prince William and Harry after Princess Diana's death? What was their reaction to Harry and William's deaths?
story: The penultimate episode of “Love Story” opens in 1997 with the death of a shocking and beloved public figure: Princess Diana.

Diana, fresh off her divorce from the current King Charles, was killed in a car accident that also took the lives of Dodi Fayed, her boyfriend at the time, and driver Henri Paul. She was 36 years old.
In Episode 8 of “Love Story,” which aired March 19, John F. Kennedy Jr. (Paul Anthony Kelly) and his wife, Carolyn Bessette Kennedy (Sarah Pidgeon), react to the news.

Carolyn is distraught in the show, watching the news with her jaw dropped. "They killed her. She did everything right. Posed for every photo. Gave them everything they wanted. And they still killed her," Carolyn says.

She later tries to talk to John about the loss that defined his own childhood. John’s father, President John F. Kennedy, was assassinated in 1963 just days before JFK Jr.'s 3rd birthday. John resists the comparison.

"This is nothing like what me and Caroline went through. They're princes. They're members of a monarchy. I was 3 when my dad died. I grew up 60 blocks from here. I rode my bike to school. It's night and day," he said.
John, meanwhile, is more distraught about how Diana's death might stop Carolyn from "living her life."
“Once Upon a Time,” the Bessette Kennedy biography that inspired the book, touches on how the couple may have actually reacted to the tragedy. Here’s what to know.
How Did JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette React to Diana’s Death?
According to "Once Upon a Time" author Elizabeth Beller, Diana’s death affected Bessette Kennedy deeply. She thought of her own position, newly married and hounded by paparazzi.

“Just a month earlier, Carolyn had sat behind Diana at Gianni Versace’s funeral, whose death, it could be argued, was also a result of his fame. She couldn’t help but wonder if a similar fate would befall her. Carolyn was terrified,” Beller wrote in the 2024 biography.
Kathy McKeon, Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis’ former assistant and her children's former babysitter, described a dinner she had with the couple the night before Diana’s funeral.

Bessette Kennedy, she said, described being chased by a “wolf pack” of photographers. “While Carolyn was forthcoming about her despair concerning the circumstances of Diana’s death, John remained quiet for the most part, though he was affected,” Beller wrote.
Christopher Andersen wrote in "The Good Son," his biography of JFK Jr., that Diana's death "served to harden Carolyn's resolve not to have children anytime soon."

Andersen quoted JFK Jr.'s friend Billy Noonan, who recalled how Bessette Kennedy reacted. "I'm not sure what I'm going to do about Carolyn," Kennedy told Noonan, per the book. "She's really spooked now."
Did JFK Jr. Call Princes William and Harry?

In her biography, Beller said that Bessette Kennedy "tried to get” her husband to call Diana’s sons, Prince William and Prince Harry, to give his condolences, after reports came out that Diana had wanted her sons to take after JFK Jr.
Tina Brown wrote, in a story for The New Yorker published after Diana’s death in August 1997, about a lunch she had with the Princess of Wales in June.
During the lunch, Diana brought up JFK Jr. as a role model for her sons and a picture of how to handle life in the spotlight.

“All my hopes are on William now,” Diana reportedly Brown. “I don’t want to push him. Charles suggested he might go to Hong Kong for the handover, but he said, ‘Mummy, must I? I just don’t feel ready.’ I try to din into him all the time about the media — the dangers, and how he must understand and handle it. I think it’s too late for the rest of the family. But William — I think he has it. I think he understands. I’m hoping he’ll grow up to be as smart about it as John Kennedy, Jr. I want William to be able to handle things as well as John does.”
In the book "The Day John Died," biographer Christopher Andersen said JFK Jr. didn’t see a parallel between him and the princes.
“I was able to lead a normal life from about the age of 5,” Andersen quoted Kennedy as saying.
Bessette and Kennedy died in a plane crash, along with her sister Lauren Bessette, in July 1999, two years after Diana’s fatal accident.

title: 'Off Campus' Hockey Romance Series Gets Premiere Date at Prime Video
summary: The next buzzy book-to-screen hockey romance drama is about to hit screens, when "Off Campus" lands at Prime Video.
story: The next buzzy book-to-screen hockey romance drama is about to hit screens, when "Off Campus" lands at Prime Video.
"Off Campus," based on the book series of the same name by Elle Kennedy, will premiere on the streaming platform on May 13.

Described by Prime Video as a "college soap," the show will focus on the hockey team at Briar University, a fictional school set in Boston in the books, and their various romantic entanglements. Season 1 is set to follow the plot of the first book in the series, "The Deal," about star player Garrett Graham and a singer/songwriter Hannah Wells.
Garrett will be played by Belmont Cameli, and Hannah by Ella Bright.

In addition to the premiere date, Prime Video also released a teaser trailer for the show, which previews Garrett's party boy attitude, Hannah's studiousness and the steamy — literally —chance encounter that will bring them together.
"Off Campus" was already renewed for Season 2 in February, months before Season 1 premieres.
The second book in Kennedy's series, titled "The Mistake," follows John Logan's (Antonio Cipriano) "second-chance" romance with freshman Grace.

There are more than 10 books total in Kennedy's collegiate hockey universe. After the four books in the "Off Campus" series, she returned to the fictional team for a set of four more, called "Briar U." In October 2023, she kicked off a third set of hockey romances in a series called "Campus Diaries."
The first book in that series, "The Graham Effect," introduced the next generation of Briar U students, including Garrett and Hannah's hockey-playing daughter, Gigi.
Her most recent Briar U novel, "Love Song," which just published March 17, followed the romance between Garrett and Hannah's son Wyatt and John Logan's daughter Blake.

title: Is Carole Radziwill In ‘Love Story’? What She's Said About the Show
summary: Is Carole Radziwill in "Love Story?" Here's what she has said about the series and her relationship with JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy.
story: While viewers can’t get enough of the romance between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy on FX’s “Love Story,” they haven't seen one person important to their story.

Carole Radziwill, longtime “Real Housewives of New York City” cast member, was married to Anthony Radziwill, JFK Jr.'s cousin, until his death from cancer at 1999, less than a month after JFK Jr., Bessette Kennedy and her sister, Lauren Bessette, died in a plane crash off the coast of Martha’s Vineyard.

Carole Radziwill attended the Kennedy-Bessette wedding, notably sharing intimate photos from the nuptials with CNN in August 2025, and wrote about her grief over the loss of her husband, his cousin and her friend Bessette in a 2007 memoir, "What Remains." But she has not been portrayed in the series at all to this point.
Anthony Radziwill is played by Erich Bergen in “Love Story." His cancer diagnosis was a major plot point of the Episode 7, which aired March 12.

Carole Radziwill was a big presence in the life and death of JFK Jr. and Bessette Kennedy. She gave a reading for Bessette Kennedy at her memorial service in Greenwich, Connecticut, per a program from the service shared by Christ Church Greenwich.
Though she's not appeared in the show, Carole Radziwill has shared her thoughts on the dramatization.
“No, I won’t be watching. But you should—watch it, and fall in love with them the way we all did back then. Just… don’t DM me, ; )” she wrote on her Substack in February.
What Was Carole Radziwill’s Relationship to John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy?
Carole Radziwill was married to Anthony Radziwill, the son of Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis’ sister, Lee Radziwill.
Anthony Radziwill served as the best man at the wedding of JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy, as depicted in “Love Story.”
Carole Radziwill and Bessette Kennedy were close.

“The real woman was neither a slave to fashion nor timid. She was a protector—the one who could crack open a desperate moment with a perfectly timed quip, fierce enough to make you rethink everything, soft enough to take your hand and guide you through anyway,” Carole Radziwill recently wrote on her Substack.
Carole Radziwill previously said Bessette Kennedy called her from the plane before the crash.

“Carolyn called me from the plane right before they were taking off,” she said in a 2017 episode of “How It Really Happened." “I don’t remember anything really important from that conversation. I certainly didn’t think it would be the last time I would speak to my friend.”
She also has a vivid recollection how that call ended.
“I remember at the end, she said, ‘I love you,’ ” she said. “For some reason, I didn’t say ‘I love you back,’ and that always stuck with me.”
What Has Carole Radziwill Said About 'Love Story'?
Ryan Murphy is the producer of “Love Story.” Before the show premiered in February, Carole Radziwill, didn't reveal too much when asked for her thoughts on the premise of the show.

“My book is a specific story about my life with my husband and with my best friend and cousins,” she told “Entertainment Tonight” in November 2025. “And what (Murphy's) going to do is different. And he does what he does and I do what I do. People will watch it or they won’t. I’m not going to engage in it too much.”
In her Substack post about the show, Carole Radziwill reflected on all the different iterations of the story told about JFK Jr. and Bessette Kennedy.

"First came the biographies, then the documentaries, and now the scripted TV shows—hyper-dramatic, moody lighting, and vaguely believable," she wrote. "It happened slowly at first, then at warp speed. I suppose it’s the predictable evolution of a story that keeps insisting on being told."
She wrote that as the story continues to be told, it's become "filled with details no one could possibly know, by people who weren’t there and don’t care about the truth."
"We think we own our stories. We don’t. Not in life, and certainly not in death. That is the quiet theft—slow at first, then at warp speed," she wrote.

"But now I am at peace with it all," she added. "I no longer worry about misrepresentations, false narratives, or bent truths. I don’t flinch at flat, lifeless descriptions or cornball storylines. I don’t care whether people get it wrong, get it right, or ever know what they were really like."
Why Isn't Carole Radziwill In 'Love Story'?

Carole Radziwill's absence in “Love Story” has certainly not gone unnoticed. When asked about the omission, executive producer Brad Simpson said there was a conscious effort to focus more on Bessette Kennedy’s sister.

“People’s lives are full and rich. There’s tons of people in them, and you have to make decisions,” he told TV Insider. “And a decision we made was to really focus on creating Lauren, her sister, as a character because she was often an afterthought in all of this. And so we thought it was important that, when Carolyn needed a confidant, it be her sister because they were very close.”

“There were tons of people in their lives that we weren’t able to include, including some of John’s closest friends, but we decided to make Lauren her closest confidant, and then Anthony his closest confidant, right?" he added. "You know, we’re trying to pack a lot into 43 minutes.”

“Love Story” has courted controversy for the people it does include in the series. Jack Schlossberg, the son of Caroline Kennedy — portrayed by Grace Gummer in the show — has called the show a "grotesque display of someone else’s life.”
Daryl Hannah, who dated JFK Jr. before he married Bessette Kennedy, has slammed the show for its depiction of her, as portrayed by Dree Hemingway.

“The character ‘Daryl Hannah’ portrayed in the series is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life, my conduct or my relationship with John. The actions and behaviors attributed to me are untrue,” she wrote in a New York Times op-ed earlier this month.

title: 'Big Little Lies' Is Getting a Book Sequel — and It's a Major Clue for Season 3 of the Show
summary: What is the plot of Big Little Lies season 3? What to know about the book sequel.
story: Ever since Reese Witherspoon announced that "Big Little Lies" was returning for a third season, fans of the hit HBO series have been clamoring for details. Now, we finally know more about the plot.

On March 19, Penguin Random House announced that author Liane Moriarty's sequel to "Big Little Lies," entitled "Big Little Truths," will come out this summer and will be the basis of the third season of "Big Little Lies."
Viewers may have to wait a while to find out when a third season will premiere, but the book release is right around the corner and  is slated for Aug. 25, 2026.
Below, we're sharing everything we know about the new book and Season 3 of "Big Little Lies."
What Is the Plot of 'Big Little Truths'?
Liane Moriarty's bestselling 2014 novel "Big Little Lies" inspired the HBO series of the same name.
“Big Little Truths,” announced in 2026, marks the first time author Liane Moriarty has written a sequel, according to Penguin Random House.
According to the official synopsis, the book flashes forward.

"The last time we saw Madeline, Celeste, Jane, Renata, and Bonnie, their children were just five years old. But now the kids are in high school, with all of the drama that accompanies teendom (goodbye playdates, hello drugs, sex, and alcohol),"a press release reads.

In the book, a "strange man" starts lurking around the school asking "supposedly innocent questions," then the principal receives a severed human finger in the mail. Naturally, the community "is in an uproar."

"This tightly connected group will finally have to face the full repercussions of the big little truths they have — and haven’t — shared with their kids, with each other, and with themselves," the book description reads.

For those who haven't read "Big Little Lies," the publishing house says the sequel "stands entirely on its own." It's described as "a nuanced, sharply observed exploration of contemporary womanhood, rich with insight into human behavior and social dynamics — and laced with Moriarty’s signature wit."
What Has Moriarty Said About the 'Big Little Lies' Sequel?
In a statement, Moriarty described the experience of returning to her bestselling book to write a sequel.

"It felt like I was catching up with old friends. I really hope readers experience that same sense of a reunion. Of course I’ve changed since I wrote Big Little Lies, and the world has changed, so it was fascinating to think about how these characters might have changed too especially as they deal with the challenges of raising teens," she said.
In an interview with USA Today, the author explained why she waited so long to write a sequel.

"Right from the beginning, because of the show, there was talk about writing a sequel, but I was never interested right away because you put your characters through so much and it just didn’t make sense to me to write something that followed on directly," she said. "A time jump always made sense to me."
In a case of life imitating art, Moriarty also wanted to wait for her own children, who were young when she wrote "Big Little Lies," to become teenagers.
"And there was a wealth of new material there," she said.
What to Know About Season 3 of 'Big Little Lies'
The first season of “Big Little Lies,” which debuted in 2017, was adapted from Moriarty’s book of the same name.
Now, the HBO series is returning for Season 3, which will take inspiration from “Big Little Truths.” Per Penguin Random House, the full cast is “expected to return.”
In an interview with USA Today, Moriarty said she’s “very hands off” with the show.
“I always said I only ever wanted to be an interested bystander,” she said.
However, the author said the hit show influenced the sequel, particularly Laura Dern’s character Renata.
“It’s all Laura Dern’s fault because she was so amazing as Renata, it was impossible for me to not give her a bigger role also in this book,” she said. “You couldn’t put Renata in a corner.”
Dern previously commented on the possibility of a third season of “Big Little Lies” during a 2023 interview with TODAY.com, saying “there would be nothing more fun for all of us.”
“You know, we would have the time of our lives,” she added.
In October 2025, Witherspoon appeared on TODAY with Jenna & Friends and teased the upcoming third season while reflecting on the impact the first two seasons had on the cast.
“It’s one of those shows where the cast, we’re just all so close and we text each other all the time and we’re just always hanging out with each other, so it was a really special show,” she said.

title: Where Is Morgan Metzer, Subject of 'Gaslit By My Husband,' Now?
summary: Where is Morgan Metzer of the movie Gaslit by my Husband today? Morgan spoke about her experiences in an interview with TODAY.com.
story: This story discusses sexual violence and abuse. If you or someone you know has been sexually assaulted, call the National Sexual Assault Telephone Hotline at 1-800-656-4673 or the National Domestic Violence Hotline at 1-800-799-SAFE (1-800-799-7233).
The harrowing journey of Morgan Metzer that ultimately empowered her to help other women facing domestic violence issues began in 2021 on New Year’s Day.

Metzer was beaten and sexually assaulted by a masked man in the bedroom of her Georgia home and left tied up with a pillowcase on her head. Shortly afterward, her ex-husband, Rodney Metzer, arrived to help her and was distraught over what happened.
Eventually the truth came out: Rodney, the father of their twins, was the one who had assaulted her.

Metzer’s story was dramatized in the 2024 movie, “Gaslit By My Husband: The Morgan Metzer Story.” The film, starring actor and singer Jana Kramer as Metzer, originally aired on Lifetime and has now found a new audience as one of the most-watched movies on Netflix.

“This one was a tough story to step into…emotionally heavy and deeply real, but it was also incredibly meaningful to tell and healing for my past,” Kramer wrote on Instagram on March 16 upon the film’s release on Netflix. “Getting to film this alongside my daughter made it even more special, and sharing scenes with one of my favorite scene partners, @austinnichols made the experience that much better.”

“Stories like this matter,” she continued. “Because sometimes the hardest truths are the ones that help people feel less alone. If this film reminds even one person to trust their voice and their strength, then it was worth telling.”
What Happened to Morgan Metzer?

At about 12:30 a.m. on Jan. 1, 2021, Metzer was assaulted by a masked attacker in her bedroom who struck her with the butt of his gun and tried to strangle her, according to a news release by the Office of the District Attorney for the Blue Ridge Judicial Circuit in Canton, Georgia.

The assailant zip tied her hands and then sexually assaulted her, all the while disguising his voice. He followed by putting a pillowcase over her head, dragging her to the deck of the house and leaving her there face down, according to the district attorney’s office. He told her not to move until she heard a car honk or he would shoot her.

Rodney Metzer then arrived, found his ex-wife on the deck and called 911 at about 2 a.m., according to the district attorney’s office. He appeared on the front porch looking like “a concerned family member,” per the news release.

A subsequent investigation revealed that Rodney Metzer had been the one who assaulted his wife, one day after he had come to her house and tried to reconcile with her. The couple, who shared two children, had recently gotten divorced.
Following the assault, Rodney went to his apartment, got changed  and went back to Morgan’s home to find her face down and tied up.

An investigation by the Cherokee Sheriff’s Office found a handgun in Rodney’s vehicle and his zip ties in his apartment that matched the ones used to bind Morgan, according to the district attorney’s office.

Rodney was also seen on surveillance video leaving a Lowe’s Home Improvement store with a pack of zip ties and on a separate surveillance video entering his apartment and leaving shortly after wearing different clothes.

Police also investigated his online search history and found searches for “how long before you starve to death,” “how to change the sound of your voice,” and “how long it takes to choke someone unconscious.”
The film now showing on Netflix not only depicts the assault on Metzer but also how her ex-husband gaslit her for years to the point where she was questioning reality.

Played by Austin Nichols of “One Tree Hill” in the movie, he is shown telling little lies to get Metzer to question reality, like saying he had told her to pick up their children from school when he hadn’t. In another scene, he pushes her down the stairs and later convinces her that she pushed him down the stairs instead.
“I want them to know it’s not big stuff. It could be very minimal,” Morgan Metzer told TODAY.com about gaslighting in 2024. “It could add up … piece by piece.”
“Check yourself with every argument or every situation that arises,” she continued. “Generally go, ‘OK, was I at fault? Did I not do this?’ And then don’t let it chip away until it gets big.”
Kramer, who has spoken about experiencing domestic violence, described the feeling of being gaslit.

“Manipulation is the biggest thing in gaslighting,” she told TODAY.com in 2024. “They can manipulate you to think anything, and it’s going to turn into guilt. So then typically you hide from your guilt. You don’t want to share your problems out loud. You are not going to go and tell somebody, ‘Oh, my husband and I did this.’ So then you keep it all inside, and you think you’re the bad person.”

Assistant District Attorney Rachel Ashe called Rodney Metzer “a master manipulator” in the news release and said he also faked a cancer diagnosis a few days before the attack to try to gain his ex-wife’s sympathy.
Where Is Morgan Metzer Now?
Metzer has been sharing her story with the hope of helping others in abusive relationships.

“If I don’t take the bad that came of this, that I feel that God wants me to do, it’s like, why did it happen,” Metzer told TODAY.com in 2024. “It would happen for no reason. I’ve got to take the pain and turn that into something that’s powerful to help women.”
One struggle was feeling like she was all alone in trying to break free from her ex-husband.

“It feels very isolating when you’re in a gaslighting abusive relationship that you’re the only one, or you’re crazy or it’s your fault,” she told TODAY.com. “And I hope people find their voice after watching this to know that they’re not alone and that there is help out there.”
She also advocates for women opening up to one another about what’s honestly going on in their relationships.

“The biggest thing is women need to trust each other,” she said. “Stop trying to make it look like everything is absolutely perfect. You’ve got the perfect husband, you’ve got the perfect life. You’ve got the perfect kids. If you would speak to each other, your friends would see the signs first, and you could get out earlier.”
Where Is Rodney Metzer Now?
He pled guilty in 2021 to 14 charges, including kidnapping, sexual battery and aggravated assault, according to a news release.
He was sentenced to 70 years in prison, with the first 25 years to be served in confinement and the remaining 45 years on probation.
Metzer is currently incarcerated in Baldwin State Prison in Milledgeville, Georgia, according to inmate records from the Georgia Department of Corrections.

title: Val Kilmer to Star in New Film Nearly 1 Year After Death Using AI
summary: Val Kilmer’s fans will be able to watch the actor perform onscreen again, more than a year after his death, thanks to pioneering use of AI technology.
story: Val Kilmer’s fans will be able to watch the actor perform onscreen again, more than a year after his death, thanks to pioneering use of AI technology.

The late actor will star as a Father Fintan, a Catholic priest and Native American spiritualist who was a historical figure at the San Juan Mission in New Mexico, in “As Deep as the Grave,” the upcoming film by writer and director Coerte Voorhees, First Line Films announced in a press release.
Val Kilmer’s role in the movie, which is scheduled to be released later this year, is being heralded as the first movie performance enabled by generative AI, according to the press release.
Voorhees revealed he originally cast Val Kilmer, who died of pneumonia in April 2025 at the age of 65, as Father Fintan five years ago.

“When Val came onboard the project five years ago, he immediately identified with the historical southwestern spiritual character of Father Fintan, and understood the importance of elevating awareness of Ann Morris’s incredible story as the first female archaeologist in North America,” Voorhees said in the press release. “It was very unfortunate that his health at the time prevented him from playing this role which spoke to him spiritually and culturally.”

Voorhees added, “We are honored to collaborate with his daughter Mercedes, who brings her own filmmaking experience, to bring this character to life in the way that we had all originally imagined it.”
A statement from Mercedes Kilmer was also included.
“My father was a deeply spiritual man and this story of discovery and enlightenment in the American Southwest and his unique role in it really resonated with him,” she said in the press release.

The late actor’s daughter said her father would be pleased to appear in AI form in the movie, which depicts the excavations of southwestern archaeologists Ann Morris (Abigail Lawrie) and Earl Morris (Tom Felton) in Canyon De Chelly in Arizona while shining a light on the history of the Navajo people.

“He always looked at emerging technologies with optimism as a tool to expand the possibilities of storytelling. This spirit is something that we are all honoring within this specific film, of which he was an integral part,” Mercedes Kilmer continued.
Voorhees told Variety Val Kilmer was too sick with throat cancer when he was cast.

“He was the actor I wanted to play this role. It was very much designed around him,” said Voorhees. “I was looking at a call sheet the other day, and we had him ready to shoot. He was just going through a really, really tough time medically, and he couldn’t do it.”

Though Val Kilmer never actually shot a scene for the movie, Voorhees said he received the blessing of Val Kilmer’s estate and his daughter to use generative AI so that the actor could remain a part of the project.
“His family kept saying how important they thought the movie was and that Val really wanted to be a part of this,” Voorhees told Variety.
The director acknowledged that including the late actor in the movie might be considered “controversial,” but he reiterated “this is what Val wanted.”
During his critically lauded career, Val Kilmer demonstrated his acting range in movies as diverse as “Real Genius,” “Top Gun,” “The Doors,” “Heat,” “Tombstone” and “Batman Forever.”
Val Kilmer was diagnosed with throat cancer in 2015. Though he recovered, the cancer forced him to undergo a tracheostomy, which affected his ability to speak.
He initially kept his diagnosis a secret and later used a voice box to narrate his life story in the 2021 documentary “Val.”

title: What 'Age of Attraction' Hosts Nick Viall and Natalie Joy Have Said About Their Age Gap
summary: Who are Nick Viall and Natalie Joy? Find out about the hosts of Age of Attraction including how old they are.
story: Nick Viall and Natalie Joy were chosen to be the hosts of Netflix’s “Age of Attraction” for a reason. The reality series follows couples dating without age in mind. At 18 years apart, Viall and Joy have a significant age gap themselves.

Viall tried to find love on reality TV before. He was in two seasons of “The Bachelorette,” one of “Bachelor in Paradise” and then helmed “The Bachelor.” Ultimately, though, he met Joy through an Instagram DM.
Joy used to work as a surgical technologist, a health professional who assists doctors in the operating room, and also as a model.
Read on for a timeline of the pair’s relationship, from their first DMs to the birth of their child.
July 2020: Viall and Joy Start Dating
The pair met when Joy messaged Viall on Instagram. In a July 2022 interview with E! News, Viall detailed the message and their first interactions.

“I think DMs are a great place to meet people,” he said. “My current girlfriend slid in my DMs. Real clever, it said, ‘You’re unreal.’ I guess it was funny. I was more curious, wondering why I was unreal.”
While the pair kept their relationship under wraps for a few months, they later shared that they officially began dating in July 2020.
October 31, 2020: The Pair Soft-Launch Their Relationship via Halloween Costume
After hinting at their relationship through social media posts throughout the month, Joy and Viall dressed up as Lois Lane and Superman for Halloween.
Joy captioned an Instagram post of her costume, “Fingers crossed i see superman tonight.” Viall later posted in a superman costume.
February 8, 2021: Viall Speaks About His Relationship Publicly
On an episode of his podcast, “The Viall Files,” Viall spoke about his relationship, without naming Joy. He confirmed, “I’m in a relationship. It’s fun. It’s great. I’m super happy.”
“I have those moments of gratitude and thankfulness,” he continued. “There’s moments where I’m like, ‘You’re really great and I’m really happy you’re a part of my life.’”
February 12, 2021: Joy Posts Viall for the First Time
In an Instagram post, Joy shared her excitement to finally be open about their relationship. She posted a sweet selfie of her and Viall.

“It's a complete weight off of my shoulders to finally announce that i am in fact the mastermind behind the plant wall,” she joked in the caption. She wrote this in reference to Viall’s backyard remodel from December 2020.
March 21, 2021: Viall’s Friends Approve of Joy
Viall and Joy went on a double date with fellow “Bachelor” alums Ashley Iaconetti and her husband Jared Haibon, who met in 2015 on “Bachelor in Paradise.”
“She’s everything we hoped he’d find. Perfect complement. 💓” iaconetti captioned an Instagram post with the four of them smiling for a selfie.
May 17, 2021: The Couple Make Their Red Carpet Debut
At the 2021 MTV Movie & TV Awards: UNSCRIPTED, Viall and Joy stepped out hand in hand.

Viall commemorated the occasion with a post to Instagram of the pair on the red carpet. “This was her first time so she didn’t know to look at the camera,” he captioned the photo of Joy looking lovingly at Viall.
June 2021: Joy and Viall Become (Dog) Parents
The family expanded for the first time in 2021, when Joy and Viall adopted an Australian Cobberdog they later named Jeff, as he said in an interview with The Pet Collective.
July 2021: The Pair Celebrate Their 1-Year Anniversary
Even though they do not remember the exact date of their anniversary, Viall and Joy posted to commemorate their relationship.

“Celebrating 1 year ish, by visiting the hotel where it all started. Thankful for every moment I have with you @nnataliejjoy,” Viall captioned a sweet photo of the couple posing for a selfie at the Williamsburg Hotel in New York City.
Joy also posted to Instagram to celebrate the occasion. She shared several images of the pair and wrote, “this has been the best year of my life, and it’s because of you 🤍.”
February 14, 2022: Viall Talks About Potential Engagement
In an appearance on “PEOPLE Every Day," Viall spoke about their compatibility and a potential timeline for their engagement.
He said Joy would “love to hear” about an engagement within a year, but emphasizes that “everyone’s situations is different.”

“I think sometimes timelines can be a little bit dangerous about, you know, setting false expectations and reaching to a certain timeline and all of a sudden ... (you) have other priorities,” he said. “I just kind of tell people to just be careful about setting timelines. If they want to set a time, it’s great, just make sure it’s mutual.”
The year before, while a guest on the “Not Skinny But Not Fast“ podcast, host Amanda Hirsch asked Viall if he could see himself marrying Joy.
“I wouldn’t be in a relationship with her if I didn’t think it was possible, you know?” he said. “She’s the first person I’ve called my girlfriend, minus TV, in a long, long time.”
June 8, 2022: Viall Talks About Their Age Gap
In a June episode of the “Call her Daddy“ podcast hosted by Alex Cooper, Viall spoke about the pair’s 18 year age-gap, explaining he felt “self-conscious” at the beginning of their relationship.

“It was something I had to get over. And it was a me problem,” he said. “I always remember talking to certain friends and I would be, like, wishing that that gap didn’t exist because I would feel less nervous about it.”
He went on to explain that the age difference itself was never an issue, just his fear of how their relationship was perceived by others.

“The more I got to know her, the more I was just going to her for advice or just checking in with her. I just felt like we met each other on the same wavelength and I always felt like she was my equal,” he said. “I’m a big personality. I don’t always feel like people, regardless of age, I can connect with. And with her, I never felt that disconnect.”
January 12, 2023: Viall and Joy Get Engaged
In a joint Instagram post on January 12, Viall and Joy shared news of their engagement.
“For the rest of my life, it’s you,” Viall captioned the post, alongside a carousel of images of the pair.
The pair got engaged in Venice, California after nearly two and a half years together.
July 10, 2023: The Pair Celebrate Their Third Anniversary with a New Home
The engaged couple celebrated their third anniversary at their new lake house, according to photos to Instagram.
Viall shared earlier in the week that they had repurchased the lake house that his family had lost 32 years prior.

“After losing the house 32 years ago, I finally brought it back in the family. I had recurring dreams my entire life since we lost the house in 1990 about some how having the house back in our finally. I always woke up. Im living a literal dream. No more waking up from this one,” Viall captioned a post to Instagram to reveal the surprise.
This marked their first anniversary as an engaged couple.

“I love our family we’ve started together can’t wait to see how it grows. Thanks for 3 incredible years and for always fighting for us. Can’t wait for the rest of our lives together,” Viall gushed about his fiancée in a July 11 post on Instagram.
August 8, 2023: Viall and Joy Announce They Are Expecting Their First Child

Paired with a black and white photo of Viall holding Joy’s stomach, the couple announced they were expecting their first child. Joy captioned the Instagram post “our biggest dream came true,” and attached a picture of a sonogram.
A month later, the pair announced they would be having a baby girl, through a heartwarming joint Instagram post.
February 2, 2024: Viall and Joy Welcome Their Daughter
Viall and Joy shared that River Rose Viall war born through a carousel of images of the newborn. The girl was named after Joy’s great grandmother and niece.
“the best part of life starts now 🦢🕊️☁️💗,” Viall captioned the post.
April 27, 2024: The Pair Ties the Knot

Nearly three months after the birth of their first child and almost four years together, Viall and Joy got married in a picturesque ceremony at her family’s 300-acre farm outside of Savannah, Georgia.
“It’s a place that has always been very special to Natalie,” Viall told People in an interview. “When I first visited, I was struck by its expansive landscape and vibrant colors.”
Celebrity guests of the wedding included Jojo Siwa, and fellow Bachelor Nation alums and Viall’s close friends, Jared Haibon and Ashley Iaconetti.
Joy walked down the aisle holding baby River Rose in her arms, who was wearing a white dress and bonnet.
2025: Joy Shares Her Experience with Three Miscarriages
In a January 2025 episode of "The Viall Files" called “Going Deeper — Our Miscarriage Story,” Joy explained she was “actively miscarrying.”

“I’ve never experienced something where I feel so empty inside,” Joy said. “My sister has had 12 miscarriages, and it’s like you feel sad and you want to be there for these people, but you don’t really know the loss that they feel until you go through it yourself.”
One week later, Joy posted to her Instagram story to show her and Viall got angel wing tattoos to remember their loss.
“with me forever my sweet angel baby 🤍🪽,” Joy wrote on top of an image of her and Viall’s tattoos.
Joy shared she experienced another miscarriage on March 23, 2025.

“While mourning the loss of our second baby at the beginning of this year, I quickly and very unexpectedly found out I was pregnant again,” Joy began. “It took some time for me to accept what had happened, only to be handed another chance so easily and so fast. But eventually, I found so much happiness. We were finally in a place of complete and utter gratitude. This past week, I miscarried again.”

On August 17, 2025, she posted to her Instagram story: “Hi friends ❤️ Wanted to thank everyone for the love and support after sharing that we experienced our 3rd miscarriage,” Joy wrote. I’m working very closely with my doctors that I love and trust and eager to find out what’s going on so we don’t experience this heartbreak for a 4th time. love yall.”
February 4, 2026: Viall and Joy announce they are expecting twins
In a joyful post to Instagram, Joy announced she was pregnant again, and this time with twins.
“our rainbow babies are finally ready to join us and this time it’s TWINS,” she wrote.
The post showed two-year old River Rose in a sweater with “Big sis” written in cursive, and Viall and Joy holding pictures of a sonogram. Joy can also be seen holding her baby bump.

On Feb. 19, Joy posted a video where Viall popped two balloons to reveal the sex of their twins. When the balloons popped, pink confetti and balloons exploded from both of them, announcing the pair is expecting two baby girls.
March 9, 2026: The Pair Share Their Excitement for ‘Age of Attraction’
In an interview with Netflix’s Tudum, the pair talked about why they are so excited for the show, and how it is similar to the other hit Netflix unconventional dating show “Love Is Blind.”

“If you’re someone who wants to find love and is having a hard time doing it, maybe opening up your mind to the possibility of partners that you didn’t before could be a great thing,” Viall said. “It certainly was for me.”

“There’s not a lot of positive information out there about age-different relationships,” Joy explained. “But watching this show, you just see these beautiful relationships unfold and realize that age really doesn’t matter. It goes out the window completely. By the end of it, you’re like, ‘Oh my God, I’m rooting for them. I love them. I hope they get married and have babies.’”

title: Ryan Gosling Explains Why He Always Breaks Character on ‘SNL’
summary: Ryan Gosling spoke to TODAY about why he breaks character so much while hosting “Saturday Night Live.”
story: There are three things you can count on: death, taxes and Ryan Gosling laughing on “Saturday Night Live.”
The three-time Oscar nominee has become almost as known for breaking character while hosting the iconic late-night show as he is for his disparate roles on the big screen.
During a March 18 appearance on TODAY, Gosling was asked by Craig Melvin if "the rest of the cast (is) going out of their way to make it difficult for Ryan Gosling to keep it together?"
“First of all, one thing they don’t tell you is that when you do ‘SNL,’ you don’t sleep for a week,” Gosling said.

“(You’re) really delirious when you get there and then you’re working with people … that are intentionally trying to destroy you, and just good luck not laughing,” he continued. “I don’t know. I know you’re supposed to take your comedy more seriously. I don’t know, but it’s, I can’t do it. It’s too hard.”
Gosling, who has hosted “SNL” four times, struggled mightily to keep his cool during his most recent gig on the show on March 7.

He broke character in three sketches, including “Wedding Tradition,” when he played an oddball wedding guest insisting on clinking his glass to make the bride and groom kiss. He also laughed in “Passing Notes,” playing a principal who catches student passing notes, as well as “Cyclops,” in which he portrayed a clueless cyclops trying to solve a riddle from a pair of muses in order to get to treasure.

In addition, Gosling, who stars in the upcoming movie “Project Hail Mary,” famously starred in the now classic 2024 sketch “Beavis and Butt-Head,” in which he and Heidi Gardner cracked up when he portrayed Beavis and Mikey Day played Butt-Head during a town hall conversation about AI.
Gosling first hosted the show in 2015. During that episode, he broke out in hysterics during the sketch “Close Encounter,” where he played a man being interviewed after being abducted by aliens.

Gosling has not shied away from discussing his penchant for breaking character on “SNL,” even comparing himself to Jimmy Fallon, who gained his own reputation for laughing during sketches during his run on the series from 1998 to 2004.

“I get in trouble a lot for laughing on the show,” Gosling told Fallon on “The Tonight Show” on March 5. “People always bring you into it. So they always go, ‘You know, you laugh too much.’ You know? ‘You and Fallon!’”

title: Rex Culpepper, Son of ‘Survivor’ Stars Monica and Brad Culpepper, Dies at 28
